Title: Bottlenecks in erucic acid accumulation in genetically engineered ultrahigh erucic acid

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes erucic acid accumulation in seed oil by increasing incorporation of erucoyl groups at the sn-2 position of glycerolipids in transgenic Crambe.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Introduced LdLPAAT enabled efficient acylation of 22:1-CoA to 18:1-LPA and 22:1-LPA in GM Crambe microsomes. 2. GM Crambe showed much higher conversion of 22:1-LPA to PA than wild type, indicating functional sn-2 incorporation of erucic acid. 3. The transgene contributed to higher erucic acid in DAG and ultimately ultrahigh erucic acid seed oil.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic coexpression in Crambe; microsomal acylation assays with [14C]-labelled glycerol-3-phosphate/LPA and 22:1-CoA; lipid profiling of developing seeds
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: lysophosphatidic acid acyltransferase (LPAAT) / acyl-CoA:lysophosphatidic acid acyltransferase
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression during seed development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds analyzed mainly at 20, 27, 34, 41 and 50 DAF under controlled growth chamber conditions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated within the three-gene stack with delayed seed development, slower early oil accumulation, and lower mature oil amount per seed than wild type
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied as one component of a three-gene engineering strategy together with BnFAE1 and CaFAD2-RNAi; although effective, 22:1-LPA remained a poorer substrate than 18:1-LPA, indicating residual bottlenecks in di-erucoyl PA formation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering ultrahigh erucic acid oils because it alleviates the native sn-2 exclusion of erucic acid; should be stacked with elongation-enhancing and oleate-preserving modifications, but additional bottlenecks before 27 DAF still need to be relieved.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: cv. Mosaik not stated as source of gene
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: very-long-chain fatty acid elongation / erucic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: elongation of oleoyl-CoA to very-long-chain monounsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly produces the elongation pathway leading to erucic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: elongated acyl-CoA intermediates leading to erucoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the condensing step of fatty acid elongation that extends oleoyl-CoA toward erucoyl-CoA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme for erucic acid synthesis, but not sufficient alone to overcome all accumulation bottlenecks
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In transgenic Crambe, promotes increased erucic acid synthesis and contributes to seed oil reaching about 68 mol% erucic acid (about 71 wt%).
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coexpression of BnFAE1 with LdLPAAT and CaFAD2-RNAi previously elevated erucic acid in Crambe seed oil to ultrahigh levels. 2. The paper infers BnFAE1 is active early in GM seed development under the napin promoter. 3. Remaining limits in early-stage erucic acid accumulation suggest insufficient coordination of FAE1 with the downstream elongase reductive steps or limited precursor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic coexpression in Crambe; seed fatty acid profiling during development; prior reported transgenic phenotype referenced and integrated with current developmental analyses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific transgene expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds across 20–50 DAF under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of the three-gene engineering stack, associated with delayed seed development and slower early oil accumulation; possible metabolic imbalance/futile cycling of elongation intermediates is discussed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors speculate that premature or imbalanced FAE1 activity relative to the other elongase components (3-keto reductase, 3-hydroxy hydratase, enoyl reductase) may cause bottlenecks during early seed development.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central target for increasing erucic acid, but should be coordinated with the full elongase system and precursor supply to avoid early developmental bottlenecks and maximize flux above current ultrahigh levels.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: omega-6 fatty acid desaturase 2 silencing construct targeting FAD2
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: fatty acid desaturation / oleic acid to polyunsaturated fatty acid conversion
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oleic acid desaturation branch competing with erucic acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux toward target product by suppressing a competing branch and preserving oleoyl substrate for elongation to erucic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: oleic acid moieties in membrane glycerolipids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: reduced formation of polyunsaturated fatty acids; increased oleic acid availability
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: RNAi suppresses endogenous FAD2-mediated desaturation of oleic acid, thereby preserving oleate for elongation to erucic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Competing-branch control step rather than directly demonstrated rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to increased erucic acid accumulation in transgenic Crambe by reducing oleic acid desaturation and increasing substrate availability for elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CaFAD2-RNAi was one of the three modifications used to generate ultrahigh erucic acid Crambe. 2. Its intended role is to reduce flux from oleic acid into polyunsaturated fatty acids. 3. Together with BnFAE1 and LdLPAAT it supported accumulation of about 68 mol% erucic acid in seed oil.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic RNAi stack in Crambe combined with seed fatty acid profiling; current paper discusses its role as part of the engineered line
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-specific RNAi expression during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds across 20–50 DAF under controlled growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As part of the engineered stack, may perturb membrane lipid metabolism during early seed development; exact individual penalty not dissected in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not experimentally isolate the independent contribution of CaFAD2-RNAi in this study; included because the GM line under direct investigation constitutively contains this core metabolic intervention for the target trait.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful companion target to preserve oleate substrate for VLCFA synthesis when combined with FAE1 and sn-2 acylation engineering.
